root.docs_md:
The window and layout shell: spaces, tabs, panes, stacks, focus ring, header and
side-sheet, command-bar input, and the single CEF layout webview that composes every page.

items.name: filter_results
items.signature: pub fn filter_results(query: &str, tabs: &[vmux_command::event::CommandBarTab], commands: &[vmux_command::event::CommandBarCommandEntry], spaces: &[vmux_command::event::CommandBarSpace], pages: &[vmux_command::event::CommandBarPage], new_tab: bool, history: &[vmux_command::event::HistoryEntry]) -> Vec<CommandBarResultItem>

items.name: TRAFFIC_LIGHTS_PAD_PX
items.docs_md:
Left padding (px) reserved on the tab row for the macOS traffic
lights when the side sheet is closed.

items.name: CEF_RESERVED_HEIGHT_PX
items.docs_md:
Vertical space the CEF shell reserves in the layout above the pane.
The CEF shell puts tabs at the very top (traffic lights sit on the
left of the tab row, in the reserved padding) so no extra titlebar
strip is needed.

items.name: WINDOW_PAD_PX
items.docs_md: Default window edge padding (px). Overridable via `settings.layout.window.padding`.

items.name: TERMINAL_CEF_BG_COLOR
items.docs_md:
Default page bg color for terminal-like stacks (terminals, processes,
agent CLIs). Matches catppuccin-mocha `base` so the CEF URL row
blends with the terminal surface below it.

items.name: PANE_GAP_PX
items.docs_md: Gap (px) between split panes inside a tab.

members.name: process_id
members.docs_md:
For terminal stacks: the terminal's `ProcessId` (its handle for `run` /
`read_terminal`). `None` for browser stacks.

items.name: LayoutPlugin
items.docs_md:
Wires the layout shell: spaces, tabs, panes, stacks, focus ring, header/side-sheet,
command-bar input, and layout apply/snapshot, aggregating the per-area sub-plugins.

members.name: radius
members.docs_md:
Corner radius (px) applied across the design system: pane corner clip,
focus ring, and the CEF CSS `--radius` variable.

items.name: FocusedStack
items.docs_md:
Cached result of `focused_stack()`, computed once per frame in `Update`
after all command handlers. Read by push/sync systems to avoid redundant
tree walks.

items.name: ComputeFocusSet
items.docs_md:
System set for `compute_focused_stack`. Systems that read `Res<FocusedStack>`
should be ordered `.after(ComputeFocusSet)` in `Update`.

items.name: PendingPaneClose
items.docs_md: Marker: pane is waiting for close confirmation dialog.

items.name: ForcePaneClose
items.docs_md:
Marker: close this pane immediately, without a confirmation dialog. Used when
the pane's process has already exited (e.g. an agent CLI quit), so there is
nothing to confirm and the pane should be removed + the split collapsed.

items.name: PendingCursorWarp
items.docs_md: Signals that the cursor should be warped to the active pane once layout is computed.

items.name: split_leaf_into_two
items.signature: pub fn split_leaf_into_two(commands: &mut Commands<'_, '_>, active: Entity, split_dir: PaneSplitDirection, existing_tabs: &[Entity], activate_new: bool) -> Entity

items.name: split_or_extend
items.signature: pub fn split_or_extend(commands: &mut Commands<'_, '_>, anchor: Entity, split_dir: PaneSplitDirection, existing_tabs: &[Entity], activate_new: bool, already_split: bool) -> Entity
items.docs_md:
Return a fresh empty leaf pane beside `anchor`, to host an agent-spawned
terminal. When `anchor` is still a leaf (`already_split == false`), it is
split in two via [`split_leaf_into_two`] (its stacks move into the first
child, the returned pane is the second). When `anchor` is already a split —
either from a previous frame, or from an earlier call in the *same* command
buffer — the new leaf is appended as another child of that split.

Calling [`split_leaf_into_two`] repeatedly on the same leaf within one
command buffer (e.g. several agent `run`s dispatched in one tick) would wrap
it again on each call and orphan an empty `pane1` every time; routing the
2nd+ split through here keeps the result a clean N-ary split with no empties.

items.name: WindowGeometry
items.docs_md:
Persisted primary-window geometry, saved as a singleton entity in `store.ron`.
`position`/`size` always describe the windowed frame, even while `fullscreen`,
so exiting fullscreen lands on a sane frame.
